_doc: Registry of Pali homographs, heteronyms, and substring false-friends that have bitten counts in this program. A bare stem/substring search over any of these OVERSTATES the intended sense, often by an order of magnitude. Before locking any count on these terms, apply the exclusion_regex (to drop morphological false-friends) AND blind sense-code a sample for the remaining heteronyms (a count whose sense you have not read is not yet a count). Patterns are PostgreSQL ~* (case-insensitive POSIX). Add an entry whenever a new study discovers a trap.

entries.trap: the substring nag/naga is a heteronym AND a false-friend magnet
entries.finding: Of 10,174 canon rows with substring naga, only 159 of 935 genuine-token rows (17%) are the serpent-being; 59% of the raw nag-substring count are morphological false friends.
entries.method: Classify by referent (CODEBOOK-referent.md); do NOT count the bare substring.

entries.trap: stem sabh[aā]v catches bhava-compounds that are not sabhava; the own-nature vs adverbial-naturally sense is also unresolved (blind sense-coding FAILED, kappa=-0.08)
entries.note: Even after the mask, the remaining hits are stem density, NOT sense-resolved (own-nature vs 'naturally'); report as stem density and disclose the kappa failure.

entries.trap: stem appan catches -kappana compounds that are unrelated
upacara is canonical in the ordinary sense 'vicinity / approach / boundary / proper conduct' (esp. Vinaya); the access-concentration sense is post-canonical
entries.finding: All 14 early upacara hits are the vicinity/boundary sense; 0 are access-concentration after blind audit (kappa=1.0).
entries.method: Sense-code; the meditative sense requires the -samadhi collocation or commentarial context.

entries.trap: do NOT fold in paramaṭṭha (grasped, from paramasati, long first a) which is a DIFFERENT word
entries.note: paramattha pattern: paramatth|paramaṭṭh|parama-?atth; paraamaṭṭha (parA-) is the grasping word, exclude.

entries.intended: rasa = function (as a member of the lakkhana-rasa-paccupatthana definitional tetrad)
entries.trap: severely polysemous: taste, juice, flavour, mercury, aesthetic rasa, essence
entries.method: Use \mrasa word-boundary; count ONLY when co-occurring with lakkhana AND paccupatthana in the same passage (the catukka frame). Never a standalone realist marker.

entries.intended: kammatthana = meditation subject (the closed-40 commentarial sense)
entries.trap: canonical kammatthana means an occupation / place of work (ordinary sense)
entries.finding: 134 of 148 mula rows carrying kammatthana are Visuddhimagga; the 14 in AN/MN/DN are the ordinary 'work' sense.

entries.intended: the narrow fixed-six (chalabhinna) vs the broad 'higher knowledge' sense
entries.trap: blind wide-vs-narrow sense split FAILED (kappa=-0.14); the string is near-even canon vs commentary, but whether the commentary narrowed it to the six is UNTESTED by stem count
entries.method: Test narrowing via the discrete compound chalabhinna vs bare abhinna per stratum (queue item 4), NOT via a wide/narrow sense audit of the bare stem.

entries.trap: the apparent canon-tilt is dominated by an EDITORIAL section-title 'Theravada Vinaya' prepended to Vinaya rows (ids of the pli-tv-bi-vb-* form), not in-text usage
entries.method: Exclude editorial section-titles; the thera-alone elder-register control runs the opposite (commentary-denser) way.

entries.trap: homograph with the genitive of sant ('existing/being') and other forms; needs sentence context to disambiguate
entries.method: Sentence-context sense-coding required; flagged as the residual gloss-disambiguation tail.

entries.intended: majja = effect-based intoxicant category; sura = a specific fermented drink
entries.trap: FTS/vector search has mis-handled this cluster; majja is a category not a substance, and the alias cluster is incomplete
entries.method: Route load-bearing intoxicant counts through SQL regex, not /api/search; majja is effect-based.
